relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Trust-made justice opens peace toward the Divine through Messiah.
Grace becomes the standing place, and glory becomes hope.
Even pressure can become a path of endurance.
Endurance forms tested character, and character opens hope.
Hope does not shame because Divine love is poured into the heart through holy Spirit.
Messiah meets weakness at the right time.
Human love rarely risks death even for the righteous.
Divine love appears while we are still misaligned.
Being made just in his blood opens saving/healing from wrath.
Reconciled through death, we are saved/healed in his life.
Reconciliation becomes boasting in the Divine.
Through one human, misalignment enters, and death spreads through all.
Misalignment is present before Law, though not counted the same way.
Death reigns from Adam to Moses, revealing a regime.
The gift is greater than the trespass.
Judgment from one trespass and gift from many trespasses move in opposite directions.
Death reigned through one; grace makes life-reigning possible through Messiah.
One trespass and one righteous act reveal two fields for humanity.
Disobedience constituted many misaligned; obedience constitutes many righteous.
Where misalignment abounds, grace overabounds.
Grace now reigns through justice into age-long life.

contemplative_note: Romans 5 is regime language: death reigns, sin reigns, grace reigns. Mystically, this is the transfer from shame/death power into grace-life power.
